section_name: Width Limitation on Certain Recreational Vehicles
chapter_title: Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
(a) In this section:

(1) “Appurtenance” includes an awning, a grab handle, lighting equipment, or a vent. The term does not include a load-carrying device.

(2) “Recreational vehicle” has the meaning assigned by Section 522.004 (Applicability) .

(b) A recreational vehicle may exceed a width limitation established by Section 621.201 (Maximum Width) or 621.202 (Commission’s Authority to Set Maximum Width) if the excess width is attributable to an appurtenance that extends six inches or less beyond a fender on one or both sides of the vehicle.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 622. Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 622.903 — Width Limitation on Certain Recreational Vehicles

(a) In this section:

    (1) “Appurtenance” includes an awning, a grab handle, lighting equipment, or a vent. The term does not include a load-carrying device.

    (2) “Recreational vehicle” has the meaning assigned by Section 522.004 (Applicability) .

(b) A recreational vehicle may exceed a width limitation established by Section 621.201 (Maximum Width) or 621.202 (Commission’s Authority to Set Maximum Width) if the excess width is attributable to an appurtenance that extends six inches or less beyond a fender on one or both sides of the vehicle.

*Added by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 491, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 2003.*

subsections.text: “Appurtenance” includes an awning, a grab handle, lighting equipment, or a vent. The term does not include a load-carrying device.
“Recreational vehicle” has the meaning assigned by Section 522.004 (Applicability) .
